annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

content.text: {"number":58242,"title":"[ResourceGraph] Docs: Fill in empty sections and add key concepts for Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` **Service directory:** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58068) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed A new `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` library was added (1.0.0-beta.1) providing declarative infrastructure provisioning for Azure Resource Graph shared queries. The library exposes `ResourceGraphQuery` and a `ResultKind` enum. A working snippet for creating a shared query is present in both the README and test file. ## Gaps Found - **`Authenticate the Client` section is empty** — no guidance on how authentication is handled in provisioning libraries (typically delegated to `azd` / Azure CLI; the library itself does not authenticate) - **`Next steps` section is empty** — no links to Azure Resource Graph documentation, quickstarts, or related Azure.Provisioning.* libraries - **`Key concepts` section** doesn't document the `FromExisting` static method pattern, which is a core concept for all Azure.Provisioning.* libraries (allows referencing already-provisioned resources by Bicep identifier) - **`ResultKind` enum** (read-only output property on `ResourceGraphQuery`) is not mentioned or explained anywhere in the README 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` **1a. Fill in `Authenticate the Client` section** (after line 19, before `## Key concepts`): Replace: ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client ## Key concepts ``` With: ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client No authentication is required to define your infrastructure using this library. `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` generates Bicep templates that are deployed via the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((aka.ms/redacted) Authentication to Azure happens at deployment time through `azd` or the Azure CLI, not through this library. ## Key concepts ``` **1b. Expand `Key concepts` section** — add the `FromExisting` pattern and `ResultKind` explanation. Replace: ```markdown ## Key concepts This library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using dotnet. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates. ``` With: ```markdown ## Key concepts This library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using dotnet. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates. ### ResourceGraphQuery `ResourceGraphQuery` represents an [Azure Resource Graph shared query]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Key writable properties include: | Property | Description | |----------|-------------| | `Query` | The KQL query string to execute against Azure Resource Graph | | `Description` | Human-readable description of the query | | `ETag` | Optimistic concurrency token; omit to always overwrite | | `Tags` | Freeform resource tags | The following properties are read-only output values set by Azure after deployment: | Property | Description | |----------|-------------| | `ResultKind` | Enum indicating the type of graph query result (`Basic` or other variants) | | `ModifiedOn` | UTC timestamp of the last modification | | `Id` | Full Azure resource identifier | ### Referencing existing resources Use the static `FromExisting` method to reference a Resource Graph shared query that has already been provisioned, rather than creating a new one: ``` ResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery)); ``` ``` **1c. Fill in `Next steps` section** — replace the empty section: ```markdown ## Next steps - Learn more about [Azure Resource Graph]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - Explore [Azure Resource Graph query samples]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - Get started with [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((aka.ms/redacted) to deploy your infrastructure - Browse related [`Azure.Provisioning.*` libraries]((www.nuget.org/redacted) for other Azure services ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **2a.** Add a `FromExisting` snippet to the test file at `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/tests/BasicResourceGraphTests.cs`. Add a new test method to `BasicResourceGraphTests`: ```csharp [Test] public void ReferenceExistingResourceGraphQuery() { Infrastructure infra = new(); #region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting ResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery)); #endregion infra.Add(existingQuery); } ``` **2b.** Add the snippet placeholder in the README under the `### Referencing existing resources` subsection (see Step 1b above): ````markdown ```C# Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` currently has all required sections, but two are empty: | Section | Status | |---------|--------| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Complete | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Complete | | Getting started → Authenticate the Client | ❌ Empty — add content from Step 1a | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Minimal — expand with Step 1b | | Examples | ✅ Has snippet | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Complete | | Next steps | ❌ Empty — add content from Step 1c | | Contributing | ✅ Complete | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 resourcegraph` — injects snippet code from test `#region` blocks into README placeholders; verify README diff shows the `FromExisting` snippet populated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 resourcegraph` — no public API changes expected, but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24538617811/agentic_workflow) · ● 514.7K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58242","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-16T23:12:44Z","updated_at":"2026-04-16T23:12:44Z"}

verify README diff shows the `FromExisting` snippet populated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 resourcegraph` — no public API changes expected, but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24538617811/agentic_workflow) · ● 514.7K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58242","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-16T23:12:44Z","updated_at":"2026-04-16T23:12:44Z"}

{"total_count":1,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3292095671,"number":51705,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":true,"title":"CosmosDBAccountCapability to implement IEquatable<CosmosDBAccountCapability>","body":"## Background
This is a "sub-issue" to one raised in the Aspire repo: [Upgrading to 9.4 causes duplicate capabilities in Cosmos DB and fails deployment](https://github.com/dotnet/aspire/issues/10808).

## Request
It would be great if `CosmosDBAccountCapability` could implement `IEquatable<CosmosDBAccountCapability>`. It would make the "upserting" of CosmosDB capabilities within the Aspire framework much easier if it were simple to compare 2 `CosmosDBAccountCapability` for equality based on their `Name` property alone.","author_association":"NONE","user":{"login":"goodcoffeecode","id":1698190,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2OTgxOTA=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1698190?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/goodcoffeecode","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1178448111,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Mgmt","name":"Mgmt","color":"ffeb77","description":"This issue is related to a management package.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ4MTEx"},{"id":1353316183,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/customer-reported","name":"customer-reported","color":"3800e0","description":"Issues that are reported by GitHub users external to the Azure organization.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMzUzMzE2MTgz"},{"id":1369687945,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/question","name":"question","color":"eaa875","description":"The issue doesn't require a change to the product in order to be resolved. {"total_count":2,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4279046922,"number":58242,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[ResourceGraph] Docs: Fill in empty sections and add key concepts for Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` **Service directory:** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58068) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed A new `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` library was added (1.0.0-beta.1) providing declarative infrastructure provisioning for Azure Resource Graph shared queries. The library exposes `ResourceGraphQuery` and a `ResultKind` enum. A working snippet for creating a shared query is present in both the README and test file. ## Gaps Found - **`Authenticate the Client` section is empty** — no guidance on how authentication is handled in provisioning libraries (typically delegated to `azd` / Azure CLI; the library itself does not authenticate) - **`Next steps` section is empty** — no links to Azure Resource Graph documentation, quickstarts, or related Azure.Provisioning.* libraries - **`Key concepts` section** doesn't document the `FromExisting` static method pattern, which is a core concept for all Azure.Provisioning.* libraries (allows referencing already-provisioned resources by Bicep identifier) - **`ResultKind` enum** (read-only output property on `ResourceGraphQuery`) is not mentioned or explained anywhere in the README
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` **1a. Fill in `Authenticate the Client` section** (after line 19, before `## Key concepts`): Replace: ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client ## Key concepts ``` With: ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client No authentication is required to define your infrastructure using this library. `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` generates Bicep templates that are deployed via the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((aka.ms/redacted) Authentication to Azure happens at deployment time through `azd` or the Azure CLI, not through this library. ## Key concepts ``` **1b. Expand `Key concepts` section** — add the `FromExisting` pattern and `ResultKind` explanation. Replace: ```markdown ## Key concepts This library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using dotnet. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates. ``` With: ```markdown ## Key concepts This library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using dotnet. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates. ### ResourceGraphQuery `ResourceGraphQuery` represents an [Azure Resource Graph shared query]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Key writable properties include: | Property | Description | |----------|-------------| | `Query` | The KQL query string to execute against Azure Resource Graph | | `Description` | Human-readable description of the query | | `ETag` | Optimistic concurrency token; omit to always overwrite | | `Tags` | Freeform resource tags | The following properties are read-only output values set by Azure after deployment: | Property | Description | |----------|-------------| | `ResultKind` | Enum indicating the type of graph query result (`Basic` or other variants) | | `ModifiedOn` | UTC timestamp of the last modification | | `Id` | Full Azure resource identifier | ### Referencing existing resources Use the static `FromExisting` method to reference a Resource Graph shared query that has already been provisioned, rather than creating a new one: ```C# Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting ResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery)); ``` ``` **1c. Fill in `Next steps` section** — replace the empty section: ```markdown ## Next steps - Learn more about [Azure Resource Graph]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - Explore [Azure Resource Graph query samples]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - Get started with [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((aka.ms/redacted) to deploy your infrastructure - Browse related [`Azure.Provisioning.*` libraries]((www.nuget.org/redacted) for other Azure services ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **2a.** Add a `FromExisting` snippet to the test file at `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/tests/BasicResourceGraphTests.cs`. Add a new test method to `BasicResourceGraphTests`: ```csharp [Test] public void ReferenceExistingResourceGraphQuery() { Infrastructure infra = new(); #region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting ResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery)); #endregion infra.Add(existingQuery); } ``` **2b.** Add the snippet placeholder in the README under the `### Referencing existing resources` subsection (see Step 1b above): ````markdown ```C# Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` currently has all required sections, but two are empty: | Section | Status | |---------|--------| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Complete | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Complete | | Getting started → Authenticate the Client | ❌ Empty — add content from Step 1a | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Minimal — expand with Step 1b | | Examples | ✅ Has snippet | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Complete | | Next steps | ❌ Empty — add content from Step 1c | | Contributing | ✅ Complete | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 resourcegraph` — injects snippet code from test `#region` blocks into README placeholders; verify README diff shows the `FromExisting` snippet populated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 resourcegraph` — no public API changes expected, but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`

Docs: Fill README gaps in Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` **Service directory:** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/` **Triggered by:** commit a6078e1 (PR #58068) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed PR #58068 added the brand-new `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` provisioning library (1.0.0-beta.1), introducing the `ResourceGraphQuery` resource type for declarative Azure Resource Graph shared-query provisioning via Bicep/azd. A CI fix was then applied in commit `a6078e1`. ## Gaps Found - **"Authenticate the Client" section is completely empty** — the README header exists but has no body text; it should explain that this library is used with `azd` for declarative infrastructure provisioning and does not require a credential object at configuration time - **"Key concepts" section is too thin** — it gives one generic sentence but does not describe the primary type `ResourceGraphQuery`, its settable properties (`Query`, `Description`, `ETag`, `Tags`), the read-only outputs (`Id`, `ModifiedOn`, `ResultKind`), or how `BicepValue` / `ProvisioningParameter` work - **"Next steps" section is completely empty** — no links to related provisioning libraries, Azure Resource Graph documentation, or the Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) quickstart - **No dedicated Samples test file** — the `#region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryBasic` snippet lives inside `BasicResourceGraphTests.cs` rather than a purpose-built `tests/Samples/Sample01_ResourceGraphQuery.cs` file; the canonical convention in this repo is a dedicated samples file per feature
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### 1a. Create a dedicated sample file **Create** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/tests/Samples/Sample01_ResourceGraphQuery.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph.Tests.Samples; public class Sample01_ResourceGraphQuery { [Test] [Description("Demonstrates creating a Resource Graph shared query (https://github.com/Azure/azure-quickstart-templates/tree/master/demos/resourcegraph-sharedquery-countos)")] public void CreateResourceGraphQuery() { #region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryBasic Infrastructure infra = new(); ProvisioningParameter queryCode = new(nameof(queryCode), typeof(string)) { Description = "The Azure Resource Graph query to be saved to the shared query.", Value = "Resources | where type =~ 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines' | summarize count() by tostring(properties.storageProfile.osDisk.osType)", }; infra.Add(queryCode); ProvisioningParameter queryDescription = new(nameof(queryDescription), typeof(string)) { Description = "The description of the saved Azure Resource Graph query.", Value = "This shared query counts all virtual machine resources and summarizes by the OS type.", }; infra.Add(queryDescription); ResourceGraphQuery query = new(nameof(query)) { Query = queryCode, Description = queryDescription, }; infra.Add(query); #endregion } } ``` > **Note:** Once this file is created, remove the duplicate `#region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryBasic` / `#endregion` block from `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/tests/BasicResourceGraphTests.cs` and replace it with the plain code (no region tags) so there are no duplicate snippet names. #### 1b. Edit the README **Edit** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` Replace the empty `### Authenticate the Client` section with: ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` is a **declarative provisioning library** used to define Azure Resource Graph infrastructure in C#. It generates [Bicep]((aka.ms/redacted) templates which are then deployed by the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((aka.ms/redacted) No credential object is required when using this library — authentication is handled by `azd` at deployment time. ``` Replace the thin `## Key concepts` section with: ```markdown ## Key concepts `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` uses the **Azure Provisioning** programming model where you describe Azure resources in C# and the library generates Bicep templates. ### ResourceGraphQuery `ResourceGraphQuery` represents an [Azure Resource Graph shared query]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) (`Microsoft.ResourceGraph/queries`). Key properties: | Property | Type | Description | |---|---|---| | `Name` | `BicepValue` | The name of the query resource (required). | | `Location` | `BicepValue` | The Azure region (required). | | `Query` | `BicepValue` | The KQL query string. | | `Description` | `BicepValue` | A human-readable description. | | `ETag` | `BicepValue` | Used for optimistic concurrency. | | `Tags` | `BicepDictionary` | Resource tags. | | `Id` _(output)_ | `BicepValue` | The fully-qualified resource ID after deployment. | | `ModifiedOn` _(output)_ | `BicepValue` | Last modification time. | | `ResultKind` _(output)_ | `BicepValue` | The query result type. | ### BicepValue and ProvisioningParameter Properties accept either literal values or `ProvisioningParameter` references. A `ProvisioningParameter` becomes a Bicep `param` declaration, allowing callers to supply values at deployment time. ### Infrastructure The `Infrastructure` class is the root container. Add all resources and parameters to it; then call `Build()` to get the generated Bicep or pass it to `azd` for deployment. ``` Replace the empty `## Next steps` section with: ````markdown ## Next steps - [Azure Resource Graph documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Azure Resource Graph query language reference]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - Other `Azure.Provisioning.*` libraries: [Azure.Provisioning]((www.nuget.org/redacted), [Azure.Provisioning.Resources]((www.nuget.org/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The snippet `ResourceGraphQueryBasic` is already referenced in the README with the correct placeholder: ```` ````C# Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryBasic ``` ```` After creating `Sample01_ResourceGraphQuery.cs` with the `#region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryBasic` block and **removing** the duplicate region from `BasicResourceGraphTests.cs`, run `Update-Snippets.ps1` (Step 4 below) to inject the code into the README. ### Step 3: Verify README structure All six required sections will be present after the edits above: 1. ✅ Getting started — Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the Client (needs content — see Step 1b) 2. ✅ Key concepts — needs expansion (see Step 1b) 3. ✅ Examples — already has `ResourceGraphQuery` example 4. ✅ Troubleshooting — present 5. ✅ Next steps — needs content (see Step 1b) 6. ✅ Contributing — present ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 resourcegraph` — injects snippet code from the new sample file into the README placeholder 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 resourcegraph` — regenerates public API listings (no new public types were added but good practice for a new library) 5. `dotnet format sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** A bot-filed documentation gap issue for the new `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` library (1.0.0-beta.1), requesting that empty README sections (Authenticate the Client, Next steps) be filled in and the Key concepts section expanded with `FromExisting` pattern and `ResultKind` enum documentation.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` v1.0.0-beta.1
**Affected API:** `ResourceGraphQuery`, `ResultKind` enum; README sections
**Scenarios:**
- The `Authenticate the Client` section in the README is empty — needs guidance that authentication is delegated to `azd`/Azure CLI at deployment time
- The `Next steps` section in the README is empty — needs links to Azure Resource Graph docs and related Provisioning libraries
- The `Key concepts` section lacks documentation of the `FromExisting` static method pattern
- The `ResultKind` enum (read-only output property) is not mentioned or explained anywhere in the README

**Root ask:** Fill in empty README sections and add key concepts documentation including the `FromExisting` pattern and `ResultKind` explanation, with a corresponding test snippet.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue. The library was added via PR #58068 (commit `4773e9c`) and is missing documentation in several standard README sections.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` against the canonical `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` for section formatting reference
2. Add authentication section content (authentication delegated to `azd`/Azure CLI at deploy time, not the library itself)
3. Add `FromExisting` pattern documentation and test snippet in `BasicResourceGraphTests.cs`, then run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 resourcegraph` to inject it
4. Add next steps links to Azure Resource Graph docs, quickstarts, and related `Azure.Provisioning.*` libraries
5. Validate with `dotnet build`, `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live`, and `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 resourcegraph`
</details>
